summary: Live L63 recovery drill acceptance is now proven with 15 green rows: five D1, five D2, and five D3 rows against a disposable NTM-adopted sacrificial session.
did: Added a reusable Flywheel helper that creates a disposable NTM session and runs the live recovery drill without touching active project sessions.
Repaired the local full-substrate kill-recover-drill runtime so liveness accepts shell-parented node agents and D3 can simulate agent-dead-pane-alive behavior.
Ran the full 5x3 live drill against ~/.local/state/flywheel/recovery-drill.jsonl and validated the durable ledger.
did_not: Did not mutate protected sessions alpsinsurance, picoz, or skillos.
Did not count fixture self-test rows as live recovery proof.
Did not claim global topology-ledger health; the helper uses a session-scoped topology probe for the disposable drill session.
residuals: The runtime kill-recover-drill.sh source currently lives in the local full-substrate skill tree, not as a public reduced installer surface.
